title: Jensen Huang Just Called OpenClaw the Most Important Software Release in History
author: OpenClawNewsFactor
summary_bullets: The CEO of NVIDIA, the company that builds the hardware that powers every AI model on Earth, just stood on stage at Morgan Stanley's TMT conference and said this about OpenClaw.
Probably the single most important release of software, you know, probably ever.
Jensen Hang made that statement on March 5th, and it is not a casual compliment.
When the person who profits most from AI compute adoption calls a single open-source project the most important software ever shipped, there is a very specific calculation behind it.
And this video breaks down exactly what that calculation is.
Hangs argument is built on three data points.
Linux took 30 years to reach the download numbers that OpenClaw hit in its first few weeks.
OpenClaw is now the most downloaded open-source software in history by that metric.
OpenClaw crossed 250,000 stars this week, passing React, a project that took over a decade to build that audience in roughly 60 days.
Third, and this is the one that matters most for Hang personally, the compute multiplier.
